items.title: BHP workers to strike as mining giant hits record production

children.content: As hundreds of BHP workers prepare to down tools in a landmark strike, the miner has revealed its highest-ever levels of iron ore production.

items.title: Scars of alleged Invasion Day terror attack run deep six months on

image.altText: About a dozen police vehicles parked across a road to close it off.

children.content: A day of evidence reflecting on racism, hate and violence directed towards Aboriginal people highlights the ongoing impact of what police described as a potential "mass-casualty event" at a Perth rally.

items.title: AusAlerts could compromise 'safe phones' for women at DV risk

children.content: Regional women experiencing domestic violence are at high risk of their safe phones being discovered during tests of the new AusAlert disaster warning system.

image.altText: A phone notification with the words 'Priority AusAlert'.

items.title: Seven-kilometre artwork creates colour journey through Perth suburbs

children.content: Under the raised railway that runs through Perth's south-east is a public art project that took five months to complete and required 2,000 litres of paint.

items.title: Plumber's citizen's arrest of alleged car thief goes viral after handbrake blunder

children.content: A Perth plumber has gone viral on social media after chasing down and restraining an alleged car thief after a botched getaway, but a prominent lawyer warns citizens' arrests are risky.

items.title: Why Australians shouldn't panic about fuel prices as war in Iran escalates again

children.content: With the price of oil rising and the fuel excise cut due to end, drivers are facing twin threats when it comes to the price of fuel, but experts say Australia is in a much better position compared with when the Middle East conflict began.

items.title: Liberal HQ says young voters don't remember Howard in 'reality check' to base

items.title: Man's death in Bali leaves trail of debt, scores of customers in limbo

children.content: Cameron Hughes, 39, died in a Balinese immigration detention centre last week under yet-to-be-confirmed circumstances. The ABC can reveal the Perth businessman left behind hundreds of thousands of dollars in unfinished business, with dozens of customers potentially out of pocket.

items.title: Scale of Australia's shortage of child psychiatrists revealed in new report

children.content: Experts are warning younger children are presenting with increasingly complex mental health needs as a new report predicts a nationwide shortfall of child and adolescent psychiatrists.

items.title: 'No white flag' in by-election race with One Nation, Liberal leader says

children.content: WA's Liberal leader says "there is no white flag being waved" in the upcoming Secret Harbour by-election, but states any result other than Labor retaining the seat, namely a win by One Nation, will be a "victory" for the party.

items.title: Researchers develop laser technology to test drinks for methanol

children.content: Adelaide University researchers have developed a way to use lasers to test closed alcohol bottles for methanol, which kills hundreds of people every year.
